title: Remote links — LINK/UNLINK/EXIT across nodes
purpose: Cross-node links use LINK and UNLINK control messages for establishment/removal, and EXIT for signal propagation. When a linked process on node B exits, the EXIT control message propagates the signal to node A. Node disconnection is treated as exit reason 'noconnection' for all linked processes on the disconnected node.
task: Implement cross-node link lifecycle with exit signal propagation.

requirements.title: Cross-node link establishment and removal
requirements.spec: Route link/unlink BIFs through distribution when the target is a remote PID.
acceptance: link(RemotePid) sends LINK control to remote node
Remote node establishes the link locally
Test: link processes on different nodes, verify bidirectional

requirements.title: Cross-node exit signal propagation
requirements.spec: Exit signals for linked processes propagate via EXIT control messages.
acceptance: Process exit with remote links sends EXIT control to each remote node
Remote node delivers the exit signal to linked processes
Trap_exit processes receive {EXIT, RemotePid, Reason} messages
Test: linked processes on A and B, A crashes, B receives exit signal

requirements.title: Node disconnection as noconnection exit
requirements.spec: When a distribution connection drops, treat it as exit reason 'noconnection' for all links to processes on the disconnected node.
acceptance: Connection failure triggers 'noconnection' exit signal for all locally-linked processes that have links to the disconnected node
Test: disconnect node B, all processes on A linked to B receive noconnection exit

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement unlink acknowledgment protocol (UNLINK_ID, OTP 26+) — use basic UNLINK
SHALL treat node disconnection as atomic — all links/monitors to that node fail simultaneously
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
